constraints: Do not modify files; do not run broad --all (task source constraint).

Registry-closure coupling: check_profile.py asserts every profile kernel_checks id is in KERNEL_DISPATCH, every rule key is in RULE_RUNNERS, and core.yaml path_allowlist enumerates EVERY profile file. Any new profile file must be added to core.yaml path_allowlist AND pass self-test closure, so profile-splitting Buildings cannot have fully parallel write scopes over support/checkers/profiles/** + core.yaml — they share core.yaml.
--all globs every profiles/*.yaml, so a 'live provider' profile cannot sit in profiles/ without being pulled into the deterministic suite.

Observed-divergence: deep file bodies (check_profile.py, core.yaml, several profiles) were read from /Users/smith/projects/BRICK while cwd is /Users/smith/.brick/worktrees/struct-surgery-0623; the two trees differ (preset count 27 vs 28). Diet Buildings must pin to one canonical checkout.
missing_scope: Per-line cost/runtime weight of each profile is not measured (no execution allowed); 'heavy' is inferred from file size and folded-profile count, not timing.
case_runners.py and kernel_checks.py were inspected only via the KERNEL_DISPATCH map and grep, not line-by-line; exact per-case fixture counts are not enumerated.
Whether any profile actually performs a NETWORK call was not proven by execution; it is inferred from docstrings stating mocks/in-process only.
The intended target end-state profile count for the diet is not declared by the task source.
not_proven: Profile execution cost/timing — no profile was run (constraint); 'heavy' is structural inference, not measured runtime.
That no profile makes a live network call — inferred from docstrings (mocks/in-process), not from execution.

Disjointness of B1/B2 write scopes is only partial: both must touch support/checkers/profiles/core.yaml (path_allowlist) and share the registry-closure invariant, so true parallel isolation is not proven — sequential (portfolio) ordering is the safer shape.

Tree divergence between /Users/smith/projects/BRICK and the cwd worktree (preset count 27 vs 28) means some deep bodies were read from a non-cwd tree; classification holds structurally but exact line counts/ids should be re-pinned on the canonical checkout.
Source truth, success, quality, and Movement are not judged here.
Per profile, the DECLARED skill manifest offered to this agent does not prove which skills were actually fetched.
returned.objective_summary: Produce a read-only diet design for the checker/profile surface after P5/P7: classify all 24 support/checkers/profiles/*.yaml by function, name the heavy/duplicative bundles and why, separate deterministic fixture checks from any live-provider checks (gemini-api vs gemini-local), state which profile owns four-llm preset completion, and propose 3-5 disjoint-scope follow-on Buildings. No files are edited; output is support evidence only.

No profile in --all is observed to make a live network/provider call. Live 4-provider execution is declared not_proven in the four-llm preset's own proof_limits.

Profiles that should NOT own four-llm completion: the runtime/live-smoke profiles must not attempt real 4-provider execution — that stays out of --all and is not_proven.
